title: quercus robur var. globulata
intro: Quercus robur var. globulata, also known as the pedunculate oak, is a variety of the English oak tree, characterized by its globular acorns. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Quercus robur var. globulata plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Quercus robur var. globulata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be found in various forms of dietary supplements.
